topic: FIELD-tier partial identification of the LEAKY FRONT-DOOR model with binary X, binary M, bounded Y, finite-support shared U, clean mediator U independent of M given X, and a scalar bounded direct leak |phi(m,1,u)-phi(m,0,u)|<=lambda.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp identified interval I(lambda) for the binary leaky front-door total effect under a single shared-U direct-leak bound, and when is it strictly smaller than decomposed direct-path sensitivity boxes?

source_refs.claim: Pearl's front-door functional point-identifies causal effects when diagrams imply the observed distribution suffices for identification; the leaky model asks what remains when the exclusion path fails in a bounded structural way.
source_refs.author: Pearl

source_refs.claim: The generalized front-door criterion identifies a population intervention indirect effect even without excluding all direct exposure-outcome paths, but it is not a sharp total-effect leak-bound set.
source_refs.author: Fulcher, Shpitser, Marealle, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Ding and VanderWeele give sharp nonparametric mediation sensitivity bounds for unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding, a different violation from a clean mediator plus bounded shared-U direct leak.
source_refs.author: Ding and VanderWeele

source_refs.excerpt: proxy information and the unknown mediator dependence structure need not decouple
must not confuse the outer LP with a sharp identified set

open_problems.why_unsolved: The front-door literature anchors the lambda=0 point-identification corner, while generalized front-door work changes the estimand to an indirect effect and mediation sensitivity bounds parameterize mediator-outcome confounding rather than a clean mediator with a bounded direct structural contrast. The prior front-door multi-mediator proposal flags the same non-rectangularity obstacle: shared latent coordinates can make an outer product of marginal intervals nonsharp, so a leaky-front-door interval cannot be assumed to decompose into a Pearl functional plus an independent direct-path box.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp endpoint theorem for I(lambda), including finite-support latent sharpness and a strict-inclusion counterexample against decomposed direct-effect sensitivity boxes.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing moves from a familiar mediation estimand to sharp bounds under one explicitly named violation.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: front-door point ID at lambda=0 -> a bounded direct path violates exclusion while the mediator remains clean -> sharp total-effect bounds for I(lambda)

method_exemplars.technique: Recover the front-door functional as the lambda=0 face, then show how the latent completion constraints perturb that functional.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed interval must collapse exactly to Pearl's front-door expression when the direct leak is zero.

method_exemplars.technique: Encode discrete latent assumptions and conditional-independence restrictions as linear constraints, then prove sharpness by feasible latent completions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The leaky front-door model is also a discrete latent-variable partial-ID problem with conditional-independence constraints.

method_exemplars.technique: Use criterion-based confidence regions for set-identified parameters after the endpoint map is represented by inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A downstream estimator must cover an interval-valued causal estimand rather than a single plug-in front-door value.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the leaky front-door endpoints be reduced to a closed-form finite-vertex formula, with explicit kink regimes where outcome-compatibility constraints switch from slack to binding?

source_refs.claim: Balke and Pearl use finite response-type enumeration and linear programming to derive tight causal bounds in a nonparametric IV model.
source_refs.author: Balke and Pearl
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Discrete measurement-error and conditional-independence assumptions can be expressed as linear constraints to obtain sharp factual and counterfactual bounds by linear programming.
source_refs.author: Finkelstein, Adams, Saria, and Shpitser

source_refs.excerpt: Add a rational 2x2 toy LP witness candidate for Conjecture 1
whether one proxy equation can create the interior endpoint

open_problems.why_unsolved: Balke-Pearl style LP sharpness is the natural method, but the leaky front-door restriction is a bounded contrast on phi(m,x,u) tied to the same P(U), P(U|X=0), and P(U|X=1) across all four outcome equations. The prior reviewer specifically asked for a rational small LP witness because shared latent/proxy equations may create interior active couplings; that is exactly the obstacle to reading endpoints off independent mediator-specific boxes.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite primal-dual LP characterization whose extreme points yield explicit endpoint formulas and regime inequalities for every slack, binding, and kink case.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the canonical narrative for turning nonidentification plus discrete structural assumptions into sharp finite bounds.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: nonparametric causal model with latent response types -> observed data impose linear restrictions -> sharp endpoint formulas from LP vertices

method_exemplars.technique: Enumerate latent response types, solve the primal LP, and certify tightness with dual inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The leaky front-door endpoint problem has the same finite latent-response sharpness geometry, but with clean-mediator compatibility constraints.

method_exemplars.technique: Translate conditional-independence and measurement restrictions into a linear constraint system before optimizing counterfactual targets.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It gives a modern LP template for discrete latent partial ID with conditional-independence restrictions.

method_exemplars.technique: Apply the directional delta method to a max/min endpoint functional and avoid the ordinary bootstrap at kink faces.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Finite-vertex endpoint formulas are piecewise linear, so regime switches produce directional rather than full differentiability.

open_problems.open_problem: What uniformly valid inference is possible for estimated leaky front-door endpoint processes lambda -> [L(lambda), U(lambda)] at singleton collapse, tie, and active-face kink regimes?

source_refs.claim: Set-identified econometric models require confidence regions that account for parameter-set uncertainty rather than ordinary point-estimator uncertainty.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Hong, and Tamer

source_refs.claim: For directionally differentiable functionals, the standard bootstrap is generally inconsistent unless the functional is fully differentiable.
source_refs.author: Fang and Santos

source_refs.claim: Proxy partial-ID bounds are nonsmooth observed-data functionals, so inference uses smooth approximation and bootstrap intervals.
source_refs.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: cross-fitted plug-in estimators of the finite-dimensional moments
separate test-inversion extension at nonsmooth faces
confidence-band target as either lower-envelope coverage, set-valued frontier coverage

open_problems.why_unsolved: The endpoint map for I(lambda) is expected to be a max/min of finite LP optima in nuisance-estimated cell probabilities. Away from active-face changes it may admit regular cross-fit delta-method inference, but at lambda=0 the set collapses to Pearl's singleton and at positive lambda constraints can tie or become slack/binding. Prior proposals explicitly left nonsmooth-face and confidence-band targets unresolved, while the published inference literature shows why ordinary bootstrap and pointwise delta arguments are not enough.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform weak limit and confidence-band theorem for cross-fitted endpoint estimators over lambda, with separate directional formulas at collapse and active-face kink regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: It cleanly frames the failure mode of ordinary bootstrap inference for kinked plug-in maps.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: estimated primitive moments -> nonsmooth endpoint functional -> directional limit law and valid resampling

method_exemplars.technique: Use the extended delta method for directionally differentiable maps and a corrected resampling scheme.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Leaky front-door endpoints have max/min and active-set kinks, the setting Fang-Santos is designed for.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct confidence regions for set-identified models through criterion functions and inequality representations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target is an interval-valued causal parameter whose coverage target must be defined at the set level.

method_exemplars.technique: Smooth nonsmooth partial-ID bounds and bootstrap the smoothed approximants.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is a causal proxy partial-ID inference paper where the endpoints are nonsmooth observed-law functionals.

open_problems.open_problem: When do proxy/proximal or hidden-mediator partial-ID bounds coincide with, strictly contain, or fail to compare with the clean-mediator leaky front-door interval I(lambda)?

source_refs.claim: Hidden-front-door methods extend front-door identification to hidden mediators measured by proxies, a different regime from an observed clean mediator with bounded direct leakage.
source_refs.author: Ghassami, Yang, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Proxy variables can partially identify causal effects without completeness, but the resulting bounds use proxy information about hidden factors rather than clean front-door mediator compatibility.
source_refs.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: observational equivalence in a full proximal latent law may impose restrictions
construct a law or bridge perturbation attaining each Hilbert endpoint
product rectangle visibly contains unattainable points off the segment

open_problems.why_unsolved: Proxy/proximal papers use proxies to recover or bound hidden confounding or hidden mediators, and their main obstacle is completeness or bridge attainability. The leaky front-door model instead observes the mediator and assumes it is clean conditional on X; the remaining uncertainty is the shared-U direct structural contrast. Prior proximal proposals flagged that algebraic bridge endpoints may not be attainable by a full latent law, which is the same kind of completion issue needed to compare proxy bounds with clean-mediator leaky-front-door bounds.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A comparison theorem giving necessary and sufficient conditions for equality, strict inclusion, or incomparability between proxy/proximal partial-ID intervals and I(lambda), plus finite counterexamples for the strict cases.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames partial identification as the fallback when bridge completeness or proxy relevance is insufficient for point ID.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: proximal point ID requires bridge completeness -> completeness is not empirically testable -> compare proxy bounds with clean-mediator leak-bound front-door structure

method_exemplars.technique: Use hidden-mediator/front-door bridge equations to translate between mediator information and latent restrictions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest paper at the proxy/front-door interface, even though its mediator is hidden rather than observed and clean.

method_exemplars.technique: Characterize proxy-driven bounds without completeness and study their nonsmooth endpoint maps.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired comparison is between two partial-ID intervals for causal effects under different latent-information structures.

method_exemplars.technique: Use smooth approximations to nonsmooth proxy-bound endpoints as an inference template after the comparison identifies endpoint maps.
method_exemplars.why_closest: If the intervals are comparable through endpoint transformations, its causal partial-ID inference machinery is the nearest available template.

literature_map:
Pearl (1995, Biometrika, Pearl1995CausalDiagrams) is the lambda=0 anchor: graphical front-door assumptions yield an observed-law functional for a causal effect.
Fulcher, Shpitser, Marealle, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2017, arXiv/stat.ME, FulcherShpitserMarealleTchetgen2017GeneralizedFrontDoor) generalize front-door toward population intervention indirect effects and allow a direct component, but not a sharp total-effect interval indexed by a structural leak radius.
Ding and VanderWeele (2016, Biometrika, DingVanderWeele2016SharpMediationSensitivity) give sharp mediation sensitivity bounds for unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding; this is a useful foil because the leaky front-door model keeps the mediator clean and bounds the X->Y leak instead.
Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA, BalkePearl1997Bounds) provide the finite response-type LP template for sharp causal bounds.
Finkelstein, Adams, Saria, and Shpitser (2020, arXiv/stat.ML, FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2020MeasurementError) show that discrete latent assumptions and conditional-independence constraints can be encoded as linear constraints for sharp bounds.
Ghassami, Yang, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2021, arXiv/math.ST, GhassamiYangShpitserTchetgen2021HiddenMediators) extend front-door/proximal ideas to hidden mediators with proxies.
Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, arXiv/stat.ME, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPartialID) remove completeness for proxy variables and obtain nonsmooth partial-ID bounds.
Chernozhukov, Hong, and Tamer (2007, Econometrica, ChernozhukovHongTamer2007ParameterSets) is the core econometric set-inference exemplar once I(lambda) is interval-valued.
Fang and Santos (2019, Econometric Theory, FangSantos2019DirectionallyDifferentiable) is the closest general inference reference for endpoint maps with kinks and failed ordinary bootstrap.
The live tension is that no located econometric paper gives sharp finite-vertex partial identification and regular/directional inference for a front-door model whose only violation is a scalar-bounded confounded direct structural path.
The study-pipeline gap bundle was considered as augmentation; its LATE/Manski/interval-regression questions are mostly off-anchor, but its repeated endpoint-inference concern supports the nonsmooth endpoint entry above.
prior_proposal_map:
No proposal, rejected-angle TeX, or review JSON for pid_leaky_frontdoor_shared_u itself was present under doc/formalization; only state/pipeline stubs exist.
eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator_v1 supplied the strongest nearby Track-A signal: shared mediator/proxy constraints can make rectangular decomposition nonsharp, and reviewers requested rational LP witnesses.
pid_proximal_l2_bridge_v1 supplied finite endpoint formulas, collapse regimes, latent-law completion concerns, and nonsmooth endpoint inference gaps.
pid_mediation_proxy_v1 was a failed but useful warning that shared-null mediation/front-door geometry must avoid definitional projection identities; its reviewer suggested strict non-rectangular product-rectangle witnesses.
pid_emsm_breakdown_frontier_v1 supplied adjacent cross-fit sensitivity-surface inference gaps, especially confidence-band target distinctions at ties and flat faces.
eid_proximal_phase_v1 was inspected as an adjacent proximal bridge-fiber source but was not promoted because the selected survivors are closer to leaky front-door shared-U partial ID.
The study-pipeline bundle was also inspected; it was mostly LATE, Manski bounds, or interval regression and therefore not used as a primary source_ref for this topic.
